items.title: Adelaide Hills man pleads guilty to murdering mother

children.content: A man who previously denied murdering his mother at their home in 2022, and even gave evidence in his own defence, has a last-minute change of heart and pleads guilty to murder.

gallery.altText: A bald man with a beard wearing handcuffs and a plaid shirt

thumbnail.altText: A bald man with a beard wearing handcuffs and a plaid shirt

avatar.alt: A woman with blonde hair wearing a black blazer and white top

items.title: 'Panic started to set in': Woman rescued from quicksand on Adelaide beach

children.content: A relaxing sunset walk at the beach with a friend turned to panic for a young woman after her leg got stuck in quicksand, prompting a rescue from police and fire crews.

image.altText: A person wearing jeans shorts, their calf submerged in wet sand

items.title: Police scour mine shafts, drain dam in search for missing outback woman

children.content: A renewed search for 41-year-old Trisha Graf will include searching about 1,000 mine shafts and draining Blue Dam.

image.altText: A woman with blonde hair and tattoos on her arm wearing a black singlet smiles while sitting at a bar

items.title: South Australia braces for severe weather and damaging winds

children.content: The State Emergency Service has advised people to move vehicles away from trees and secure loose items around properties as South Australia braces for damaging winds.

image.altText: Two women cross the street in the rain with one protected under an umbrella and the other pulling it towards her.

items.title: Public servant to take SA government to court over golf course redevelopment

children.content: An Adelaide man alleges the state government failed to properly refer the controversial North Adelaide Golf Course to the federal environment minister for approval.

image.altText: A man wearing a green shirt standing in front of a line of trees and a dirt path while holding folders

items.title: Paddle-steamer first launched 120 years ago returns to working life

children.content: After being raised from the bottom of the river, dug out of the mud, and lovingly restored, the PS Cannally is ready to take passengers up and down the River Murray.

gallery.altText: The PS Canally is moored on a river. It is white and grey with colourful flags hung up on it.
A photograph shows logs of wood being used to raise the a boat out of a river.

thumbnail.altText: The PS Canally is moored on a river. It is white and grey with colourful flags hung up on it.
A photograph shows logs of wood being used to raise the a boat out of a river.

items.title: Roadside stalls are booming, with 45,000 members in one group alone

children.content: Nestled away in the Adelaide Hills are dozens of roadside stalls dedicated to local produce, including baked goods and bouquets, and business is booming.

image.altText: A woman with stands next to a wooden stall stocked with loaves of bread. She wears jeans and a tshirt, smiling

items.title: 'Bit of a gold rush' as companies search for underground hydrogen

children.content: It's been about for a century, but companies are springing to action to find "gold hydrogen" underground around the country, as Australia and the world strive to reach net zero emissions by 2050.

image.altText: A drill rig at night with trucks and lights surrounding it.

items.title: Australia could still buy five US submarines if needed, defence minister says

children.content: Defence Minister Richard Marles has defended a shake-up to AUKUS that would involve Australia buying three second-hand Virginia-class submarines from the United States.

image.altText: Richard Marles speaks during an AUKUS Defence ministers' press huddle

items.title: After nearly 80 years, the actors in this film have finally been understood

children.content: The "huge swathes" of Pitjantjatjara lines in the 1949 film Bitter Springs are at last translated.
Warning: This story contains images of Indigenous people who have died.

image.altText: A film poster with the words Bitter Springs plastered onto it, depicting Aboriginal men with traditional weapons

items.title: Spotlight on Welcome to Country ceremonies in wake of Anzac Day controversy

children.content: Indigenous elders explain why they believe Welcome to Country ceremonies have faced opposition, and why abandoning the practice would create a cultural "abyss".

gallery.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony
An Indigenous person stands next to the Balbuk Yooreel statue in a park
Noongar elder Aunty Tina Hayden carries out a Welcome to Country

thumbnail.altText: An Indigenous man in traditional cultural dress at a smoking ceremony
An Indigenous person stands next to the Balbuk Yooreel statue in a park
Noongar elder Aunty Tina Hayden carries out a Welcome to Country

items.title: 'Deeply concerning outbreak': Australia keeps borders open as Ebola spreads

children.content: The federal government will not impose border restrictions on travellers from Ebola-stricken countries despite new suspected cases in Italy and Brazil, as the worsening outbreak prompts other countries to take action.

image.altText: A group of workers in head-to-toe medical protective gear walk in a line as the lead worker sprays a substance on the ground.

items.title: Eerie footage and DNA: What we know four months since Nancy Guthrie vanished

children.content: It has been four months since Nancy Guthrie, the mother of America's Today show co-host Savannah Guthrie, was reported missing. This is what we know about the alleged abduction.

image.altText: A photo from instagram of savannah guthrie and her mum at a sports game

items.title: The site politicians want axed and schools want blocked

children.content: A website where your face is scored, ranked and judged in seconds is going viral.
Politicians want it scrapped, schools are trying to block it, and some of the world’s biggest streamers are helping drive its reach.
So what exactly is Omoggle?
We take a closer look at the site and its links to a growing subculture that's pushing extreme methods & reshaping how young men see themselves.

image.altText: A stylised blue portrait-aspect image shows a tight shot of a young man's face with text: breaking down Omoggle and looksmaxxing

gallery.altText: A stylised blue portrait-aspect image shows a tight shot of a young man's face with text: breaking down Omoggle and looksmaxxing

thumbnail.altText: A stylised blue portrait-aspect image shows a tight shot of a young man's face with text: breaking down Omoggle and looksmaxxing

items.title: Police unlawfully arrested this man, broke his ribs, then charged him with assault

children.content: After fracturing about a dozen of a man's ribs and puncturing his lung, police officers discussed how to explain what happened to him.

image.altText: A man winces in agony in the corner of a police holding cell as three officers stand over him.

items.title: Woman charged with joining Islamic State 'renounces' terror group

children.content: Melbourne woman Rayann El Houli appears in court for a bail hearing after being charged with travelling to Syria to join the Islamic State group, before returning home last year.

image.altText: A woman wears a black niqab in a court sketch

items.title: Almost 70,000 without power after storms tear through southern WA

children.content: Tens of thousands of homes are without power and the SES receives hundreds of calls for help, with the roofs torn off two apartment buildings, after WA's southern half is hammered by more severe weather.

items.title: 'One more to go': Aussies on cusp of winning rugby 7s World Championship

children.content: Australia puts itself in pole position to win the women's rugby sevens World Championship, while the men's team also enjoys the spoils of victory in Spain.

image.altText: Australia celebrates with the triphy after winning the second leg of the rugby sevens World Championship in Spain.

items.title: Four easy, tasty curries for busy winter weeknights

children.content: Curries are perfect for cooler months, and many come with a serving of immune-boosting properties.

items.title: New Wieambilla property owners send message about respecting the lives lost

children.content: The son of Wieambilla police killers says the new owners of the property are open to maintaining a memorial at the site to the slain officers.

image.altText: A composite image of a man and woman both in police uniforms.

items.title: Broome local cringes as AI sells WA's north 'as something it's not'

children.content: A wave of AI-generated videos posted to social media causes a stir among tour operators in WA's north. They say the content does not accurately portray the region and threatens the preservation of Indigenous culture.

gallery.altText: Bright blue water with rocks in the foreground.
This AI-generated video claims to show the Kimberley's Devonian limestone reef, with "sunbathing crocodiles".

thumbnail.altText: Bright blue water with rocks in the foreground.
This AI-generated video claims to show the Kimberley's Devonian limestone reef, with "sunbathing crocodiles".

items.title: 'Loving' parents not at fault for deaths of children left in hot cars, coroner finds

children.content: The deaths of two children who were left in cars during hot weather were the result of their parents' memory failure, a NSW coroner finds.

image.altText: Child's arm dangles while in a car seat.

items.title: $6 million of cocaine found at north Queensland boat ramp

children.content: Packages of cocaine estimated to be worth $6 million have been found floating in the sea and near the remains of a burnt-out truck at an isolated north Queensland boat ramp.

image.altText: Packages of cocaine spread out on a table.

items.title: Decades-old trees in Darwin finally flower for the first and only time

children.content: About 20 Gebang palms have flowered in Darwin in a rare spectacle, reaffirming the local council's plans to foster a new generation of the trees.

gallery.altText: A close-up shot of a Gebang palm tree in flower, with small yellow tufts growing outward.

A group of tall palm trees beside a lake.

items.title: NT WorkSafe charges health department over hospital aerosol can death

children.content: WorkSafe charges Northern Territory Health with failing to comply with health and safety duties over the death of a mental health in-patient at Royal Darwin Hospital in 2022.

image.altText: Emergency department of hospital with sunset behind it

items.title: ASX 200 finishes flat after clawing back early losses, oil higher — as it happened

children.content: The ASX 200 has finished largely flat after clawing back early losses, while oil prices rose as investors continued to monitor tensions in the Middle East. Look back on our business blog.

children.content: High school students have put on a music festival as part of their studies to get them into the industry.

items.title: Government loses bid to keep terror funding documents secret

children.content: Commissioner Virginia Bell says the documents will provide a comparison of the resourcing given to counterterrorism agencies before and after August 2024, when the terror threat level was raised to "probable".

image.altText: A woman looking intensely serious.

items.title: Victoria's anti-corruption watchdog to get follow-the-money powers

children.content: The new powers will allow IBAC to pursue taxpayer funds through subcontracting arrangements and labour-hire companies.

image.altText: Victorian Premier Jacinta Allan in front of flags for a press conference.

items.title: Beneath the rubble of Liddell Power Station towers lay 'perfect' plan

children.content: Half a tonne of explosives was needed to bring the Hunter Valley's Liddell Power Station chimneys crashing down.

gallery.altText: Two concrete power station chimneys fall, one is in half and dust is spilling from the bases.
Water spurts up either side of two concrete chimneys falling into each other

items.title: Why Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party 'caused a stir'

children.content: From the Harlem Renaissance of the 20s and 30s to Soul Food Sundays at Wimbledon in the 80s and 90s, Naomi Osaka and Taylor Townsend's dinner party before the French Open continues a long history of black athletes coming together.

image.altText: Coco Gauff, Gael Monfils, Naomi Osaka, Taylor Townsend, Asia Muhammad and Christopher Eubanks stand arm in arm.

items.title: Three-year-old child dies in Melbourne house fire

children.content: A child has died in a house fire in Werribee and another person has been taken to hospital with serious injuries.
